title: Will Starship launch from foreign shores? SpaceX 'constantly exploring' options for megarocket liftoff sites
summary: SpaceX is exploring additional launch sites for its Starship megarocket, considering locations both within and outside the United States. The company aims to expand its launch capabilities beyond current facilities.

title: What flings mysteriously powerful particles called 'cosmic rays' at Earth?
summary: High-energy cosmic rays, 10 million times more powerful than Earth's strongest particle accelerator, may hold clues to a 60-year-old mystery. These particles could reveal a superheavy secret in space.

title: Astrophotographer captures remarkable view of galactic 'Eyes' with backyard telescope
summary: An astrophotographer captured a remarkable image of a string of galaxies resembling 'Eyes' using a backyard telescope. The galaxies are part of the massive Virgo Galaxy Cluster.

title: Blue Origin's lunar lander mockup is ready for NASA Artemis astronaut training
summary: Blue Origin has completed a mockup of its Blue Moon Mark 2 lunar lander for NASA's Artemis astronaut training at the Johnson Space Center. The mockup is now ready for astronauts to begin training.

title: Lasers shine a new light on the space junk air pollution problem
summary: Scientists have developed a new technique using lasers to study space debris and its impact on Earth's atmosphere. This method aims to better understand and address the growing problem of space junk pollution.

title: The Whirlpool Galaxy comes alive | Space photo of the day for May 13, 2026
summary: The Whirlpool Galaxy (M51) is featured in a new image from the James Webb Space Telescope. The photo highlights the galaxy's stunning structure and details.

title: 'Like putting a microscope into the core of the sun': World's 1st space-based neutrino detector launches to orbit
summary: The world's first space-based neutrino detector was launched to orbit this month to study elusive neutrino particles that constantly bombard Earth. This mission aims to provide unprecedented insights into the core processes of the sun and other cosmic phenomena.

title: NASA-Supported Space Tech Advances Earthly Construction
summary: NASA-supported 3D printing technology developed for lunar habitats is being adapted by Branch Technology Inc. to create efficient and strong buildings on Earth using Freeform 3D Printing with lightweight lattice structures.

title: Rise Goes to Washington
summary: NASA's Artemis II crew, including astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA's Jeremy Hansen, showcased the mission's zero gravity indicator 'Rise' to congressional staff in Washington on May 12, 2026. The Artemis II mission is a nearly 10-day journey planned as part of NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: NASA Outlines Preliminary Artemis III Mission Plans
summary: NASA is advancing plans for the Artemis III mission in Earth orbit to test rendezvous and docking with commercial lunar landers from Blue Origin and SpaceX. The mission is part of NASA's accelerated timeline to prepare for crewed lunar landings at the Moon's South Pole.

title: Studying Pneumonia in Space for Heart Health on Earth
summary: Expedition 74 astronauts on the International Space Station are studying how pneumonia-causing bacteria may cause long-term heart damage, using stem cell-derived heart tissues to explore new cardiovascular health and infectious disease management methods.

title: NASA’s Planet-Hunting TESS Reveals Dazzling Night Sky
summary: NASA's TESS mission has released its most comprehensive view of the night sky, showcasing its planet-hunting capabilities. The image highlights the spacecraft's ability to capture a wide range of celestial objects.

title: NASA Langley Engineer Attends FAA Training
summary: A NASA Langley engineer attended FAA training to address challenges in high-traffic airport communications, where spectrum and time constraints can lead to message loss during simultaneous transmissions.

title: Perseverance Stuns in New Selfie
summary: NASA's Perseverance rover captured a new selfie at the 'Lac de Charmes' site on Mars, showcasing its mast and a rocky outcrop after creating an abrasion patch. The selfie was assembled from 61 individual images.

title: NASA’s Perseverance Rover Snaps Selfie in Mars’ Western Frontier
summary: NASA's Perseverance rover captured a selfie at Mars' 'Lac de Charmes' using 61 images. The rover's team corrected the spelling of the nearby outcrop nicknamed 'Arathusa.'

title: NASA’s Perseverance Captures Panorama at ‘Arbot’
summary: NASA's Perseverance rover captured a detailed panorama of the 'Arbot' area on Mars using its Mastcam-Z camera. The image, composed of 46 images, provides a rich geological view during the rover's exploration beyond Jezero Crater.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed construction of a redesigned launch pad for its Starship rocket. The new pad is now preparing for its first launch.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 resupply mission to ISS scrubbed due to weather
summary: SpaceX's CRS-34 resupply mission to the International Space Station (ISS) was scrubbed due to weather conditions. The delay occurred shortly after the Progress MS-34 spacecraft docked with the station.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: SpaceX and China have a busy launch schedule with nine launches planned across the United States and China during the week of May 11. The launches span multiple missions and highlight activity in both countries.

title: Full Stack Flight 12 completes WDR
summary: Full Stack Flight 12 completed a Wet Dress Rehearsal (WDR) after Booster 19's successful static fire test. The event was conducted on May 7, 2026.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station while advancing deep space missions. The country's commercial sector is also making progress toward reusability.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is undergoing conversion work as Flight 12 approaches. The process follows the conclusion of Flight 11 six months prior.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 mission, potentially marking a calm period before a busy launch period.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, carrying the CAS500-2 satellite and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for Starship Flight 12 with a revised trajectory. The upcoming mission aims to build on previous test flights.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully conducted the maiden launch of its new Soyuz-5 rocket on Saturday. The debut flight marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency (ESA) test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space. Their work ensures the success of ESA's missions by addressing contamination and environmental challenges.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space missions.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' is vague and lacks context. It may refer to a spacecraft or satellite component designed for durability and precision.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission involving a parachute deployment test was conducted, likely to ensure proper functioning during re-entry. The event highlights advancements in parachute technology for space missions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars landing by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 launch and 25-month journey to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could significantly improve space debris removal and satellite servicing missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, which includes an online preparatory session in April-May and an in-person event at ESA Headquarters in Paris on June 8-9, 2026. The forum aims to gather feedback on topics affecting small and medium-sized enterprises in the space sector.

title: Exclusive: LEAP Launches its First Rocket
summary: LEAP has successfully launched its first rocket, marking a significant milestone for the company as a new entrant in the space launch industry.

title: CBO Puts $1.2T Price Tag on Golden Dome
summary: The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) estimates the cost of the Golden Dome program at $1.2 trillion, significantly higher than the $185 billion figure proposed by Gen. Michael Guetlein in March. The discrepancy highlights major budgetary concerns for the program.

title: Exobiosphere, Voyager Partner on ISS Mission
summary: Exobiosphere has partnered with Voyager to deploy an automated mini lab for high-throughput screening on the International Space Station (ISS). The Orbital High-Throughput Screening Device is designed to fit into standard mid-deck lockers aboard crewed or uncrewed space stations.

title: NASA’s Launch Director Pick Turns Political
summary: Brian Hughes, NASA's first senior launch operations director, previously served as the agency's chief of staff but left after Administrator Jared Isaacman took over. His new role has been described as turning political.

title: Star Catcher Closes $65M Series A
summary: Star Catcher has secured $65 million in a Series A funding round led by B Capital, with Shield Capital and Cerberus Ventures co-leading the investment.

title: Fenix Space Flies Tow-Launch Prototype
summary: Fenix Space has successfully flown a tow-launch prototype, marking a significant milestone in its development. The company is advancing toward its goal of commercial spaceflight.

title: Aetherflux Rebrands, Pivots Business—and Raises $275M
summary: Aetherflux has rebranded to Cowboy Space Corporation and raised $275 million to align with its new business direction.

title: SOCOM Taps SkyFi to Build Tactical EO Imagery Tools
summary: SOCOM has selected SkyFi to develop a prototype for a sovereign intelligence platform providing troops with faster access to geospatial imagery. The platform aims to enhance tactical decision-making through improved EO (electro-optical) imagery tools.

title: Lunar Outpost Closes $30M Series B, Unveils New Rover
summary: Lunar Outpost raised $30 million in a Series B funding round and unveiled a new rover. The investment reflects investor interest in NASA's plans for lunar exploration.

title: Rocket Lab To Acquire Motiv Space Systems
summary: Rocket Lab will acquire space robotics company Motiv Space Systems, which will be rebranded as Rocket Lab Robotics upon completion in Q2. The acquisition was announced alongside Rocket Lab's Q1 earnings.

title: SpinLaunch to Collaborate with Aalyria for Network Orchestration
summary: SpinLaunch is collaborating with Aalyria Technologies to develop infrastructure for SpinLaunch's low-cost Meridian LEO communications constellation. Aalyria will act as a strategic partner in this effort.

title: Neo Space Group Selects ThinKom Antenna for Narrow-Body Installations
summary: Neo Space Group has chosen ThinKom's ThinAir Ka2517 multi-orbit phased array antenna for narrow-body aircraft installations across multiple airlines. ThinKom announced this adoption on Tuesday.

title: Eutelsat Shows Strong Growth in LEO Services and Mobility in Q3
summary: Eutelsat reported strong growth in Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) services and mobility in Q3, with LEO-driven revenue increasing by 65% year-over-year. The company's overall revenue reached 293 million euros ($343 million).

title: Bringing LEO to Private Networks: Logos Space Engineering VP Explains the Constellation’s Enterprise-Focused Design
summary: Logos Space is developing a satellite constellation focused on private, secure networks for enterprises and governments. The company is advancing its plans after receiving FCC approval.

title: Networks Business Propels Revenue Growth for SES, Operator Cancels 2 GEO Sats
summary: SES's Networks business drives revenue growth, while the company cancels two GEO satellites. Financial results align with analyst expectations.

title: AST SpaceMobile Confirms Target for 45 BlueBirds This Year, Despite Blue Origin Launch Failure
summary: AST SpaceMobile aims to deploy 45 BlueBird satellites in 2024, despite a recent Blue Origin launch failure. CEO Abel Avellan reaffirmed the target during an investor update.

title: Star Catcher Raises $65M Series A to Fuel In-Orbit Power Grid
summary: Star Catcher, led by Andrew Rush, raised $65 million in Series A funding to develop an in-orbit power grid. The startup aims to revolutionize space-based energy infrastructure.

title: TrustPoint Receives $4M SpaceWERX Tactical Funding for Independent PNT System
summary: TrustPoint received a $4 million SpaceWERX Tactical Funding Increase (TACFI) contract to develop four satellites and four ground stations for an independent Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT) system demonstration. The funding will support the full end-to-end testing of the system.

title: Space42 Enhances D2D Prospects with Skylo Partnership
summary: Space42 partnered with Skylo Technologies to enhance direct-to-device connectivity using its Thuraya-4 geostationary satellite. This collaboration aims to deliver standards-based D2D connectivity solutions.

title: Vitesse Systems and Accelint Become Part of New Defense Tech Company Lyntris
summary: Vitesse Systems and Accelint have merged to form a new defense technology company named Lyntris, which will focus on the connected battlefield. Both companies were previously owned by Trive Capital.
